question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: However, it will now reopen on May 1st to ensure the safety of all members.
question_data.Paragraph: Dear Connexa Point Table Tennis Center members, I am the manager of the Connexa Point Table Tennis Center. ( ① ) Thank you for your interest in the upcoming reopening of the center. ( ② ) Unfortunately, during the repair process, unexpected electrical issues were discovered, causing the work to take longer than we planned. ( ③ ) We regret to inform you that the center's reopening must be delayed. ( ④ ) The center was originally scheduled to reopen on April 1st. ( ⑤ ) We look forward to seeing you back on the court soon.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
⑤
*해설: 주어진 문장은 원래 4월 1일로 예정된 재개장이 5월 1일로 변경된다는 내용이다. ④ 뒤에서 원래 일정을 언급한 직후에 변경된 일정을 제시하는 것이 자연스럽다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: She thought they were too big, like monsters.
question_data.Paragraph: It was the first carnival for the four-year-old girl, Cassie. ( ① ) She had only heard about it from her mother. She had been imagining colorful balloons and delicious candies for a long time. ( ② ) When she finally arrived at the carnival, the twinkling lights and music made her jump with joy. ( ③ ) The magician showed his tricks and she cheered with her family. But suddenly, she saw a lot of huge balloons in the parade coming towards her. ( ④ ) Her heart began to beat fast from fear. ( ⑤ ) Cassie froze and dropped her cotton candy on the ground. She quickly hid behind her mother.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 거대한 풍선을 괴물처럼 느꼈다는 내용으로, 거대한 풍선을 본 직후(④ 앞)에 위치하며 이어서 두려움으로 심장이 빨리 뛰기 시작한다는 내용과 자연스럽게 연결된다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: By sharing a revealing story or a personal flaw, you allow audience members a way to identify with you.
question_data.Paragraph: There is simply no better way to influence or stir an audience than by opening up to them with a personal story or anecdote. ( ① ) To be clear: I'm not saying you need to tell them long stories about your family vacations. ( ② ) I'm saying that you can share a key biographical detail, or an emotion that you're feeling in the moment, or a self-deprecating joke. ( ③ ) It is a tried-and-tested way of bonding with an audience of strangers ─ and of laying the groundwork for you to then persuade them. ( ④ ) The harsh reality is that people won't bond with your arguments in a vacuum, but they will "bond with you" ─ the person making those arguments. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
⑤
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '드러내는 이야기나 개인적 결점을 공유하면 청중이 자신과 동일시할 수 있다'는 내용이다. ④ 뒤에서 사람들이 논리가 아닌 '사람'과 유대한다는 설명 이후, 그 구체적 방법으로 제시되는 것이 자연스럽다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Centuries ago, leaders of the native North American Iroquois people created 'seven generation stewardship,' instructing people to consider the impact of every decision on their children, seven generations into the future.
question_data.Paragraph: We are now at a point of unprecedented genetic, cultural, and environmental power as a species. ( ① ) We are embodied individuals trapped within limited time, but we are also networked data streams, memories, and influencers, and part of a grander humanity. ( ② ) Our decisions today have far-reaching impacts that place a responsibility on us to become good ancestors, to take the long view and time travel forward to imagine the well-being of billions of people. ( ③ ) ( ④ ) In the precious few decades that Earth is ours, while we enjoy the gardens planted by our ancestors, we must not steal the shade from our descendants. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '7세대 관리'라는 구체적 사례이다. ③ 뒤에서 좋은 조상이 되어야 한다는 책임을 언급한 직후, 이를 뒷받침하는 역사적 사례로 제시되고, ⑤ 앞의 '조상이 심은 정원/후손의 그늘'과 연결된다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Many who switch from processed grain foods like white bread and white rice to whole grain types, for instance, gradually learn to prefer the nutty flavors and chewy textures.
question_data.Paragraph: Despite learned eating behaviors that are formed in early childhood and inborn biological differences, taste preferences can be changed throughout our lives due to neuroplasticity. ( ① ) There is far more flexibility in our food behaviors than most think, even as we age. ( ② ) This is terrific news for adventurous eaters who want to expand their dinner menu. ( ③ ) But it's amazing news for those eager to break poor diet habits. Just as kids gradually learn to like nutritious foods, so, too, can adults readjust their taste. ( ④ ) Repeated exposure ─ and a willingness to change ─ is the key. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 가공 곡물에서 통곡물로 전환하면 점차 선호하게 된다는 구체적 사례이다. 성인도 미각을 재조정할 수 있다는 ④ 앞 문장의 구체적 증거로, '반복 노출이 핵심'이라는 ⑤ 앞 결론과 연결된다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: After that, this tendency decreases, so that by 16 or 17, young people are much more able to disagree with the group.
question_data.Paragraph: Conformity in the teenage years has been studied by putting young people in situations where they are asked to make a choice that appears to go against what everyone else in the group is saying. ( ① ) The fascinating thing about the results of these studies is that conformity is not spread equally across all age groups. ( ② ) Thus, the willingness to go along with others reaches a peak around the age of 14. ( ③ ) This is an important finding. ( ④ ) It demonstrates that the influence of the peer group is not the same across all ages. ( ⑤ ) As young people mature, they become more resilient, and more able to defend their opinions as individuals.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 14세 이후 동조 경향이 감소한다는 내용이다. ③ 앞에서 14세에 정점을 찍는다고 한 직후, 그 이후의 감소를 설명하고, ④의 '이것은 중요한 발견'으로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: This uses a tempting habit ─ listening to that awesome audiobook ─ and combines it with a habit people would like to build, like exercising.
question_data.Paragraph: Habits aren't bad. We need them to survive. ( ① ) One question to ask is, are you really trying to break a habit, or would you do better by attaching to another one (known as temptation bundling)? ( ② ) Wharton professor Katy Milkman led a research project where participants could only have access to their audio devices while at the gym. ( ③ ) The participants whose devices were 'held hostage' were 51 percent more likely to visit the gym. ( ④ ) And the really amazing thing is what happened after it was over: nearly two-thirds opted to pay to have gym-only access for their devices! ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 유혹적 습관(오디오북)과 구축하고 싶은 습관(운동)을 결합한다는 설명이다. ② 뒤 연구 설명 직후, 그 연구의 원리를 설명하고, ④의 결과(51% 더 방문)로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: In fact, there are no folders inside the computer ─ those are effective conceptualizations designed to make them easier to use.
question_data.Paragraph: A conceptual model is an explanation, usually highly simplified, of how something works. ( ① ) It doesn't have to be complete or even accurate as long as it is useful. ( ② ) The files, folders, and icons you see displayed on a computer screen help people create the conceptual model of documents and folders inside the computer. ( ③ ) Sometimes these depictions can add to the confusion, however. ( ④ ) When reading e-mail or visiting a website, the material appears to be on the device, for that is where it is displayed and used. ( ⑤ ) But in fact, in many cases the actual material is 'in the cloud,' located on some distant machine.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 실제로 컴퓨터 안에 폴더가 없다는 것을 밝히는 내용이다. ② 뒤에서 파일/폴더/아이콘이 개념 모델을 만든다고 한 직후, 그것이 실제가 아닌 개념화임을 설명하고, ④의 '혼란을 야기할 수도 있다'로 연결된다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: In North America and Western Europe, however, the attitude and policies are just the opposite: walking is efficient, healthful, and natural.
question_data.Paragraph: The principal transportation mode in the developing world, even in large cities, is still walking because of constraints on the resources needed to operate extensive transit systems. ( ① ) People cover long distances on foot every day and expend human energy that they can hardly spare. ( ② ) Walking under those conditions is an unavoidable burden that consumes productive capability. ( ③ ) We should do more of it ─ almost everybody agrees ─ and some of the current trends should be reversed. ( ④ ) Ironically, among the most popular exercise machines in health clubs and in homes are treadmills that simulate walking, which could be otherwise accomplished with a transport purpose on the street. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 'however'로 시작하며 북미와 서유럽에서는 반대라는 대조를 나타낸다. ② 뒤에서 개발도상국에서 도보가 부담이라는 설명 직후, 선진국의 대조적 시각을 제시하고, ④의 '더 많이 걸어야 한다'는 주장으로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: When there's too much to see, too much to step over, and too much input, kids have a hard time making a choice.
question_data.Paragraph: Just like how other rooms in your home can cause anxiety when filled with too much stuff, the same is true for kids. ( ① ) If the play space houses every single toy that has ever been purchased for them since birth, they may not be able to express their feelings, but they can feel overwhelmed. ( ② ) This reminds us of how women look in their closets packed full of clothes and think, I have nothing to wear. ( ③ ) Revision helps everyone see what they have and use what they own. ( ④ ) Streamlining a play space is so important. ( ⑤ ) You want your kids to feel inspired and imaginative in the room ─ not overcome with indecision.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 너무 많은 것이 있으면 선택이 어렵다는 내용이다. ③ 뒤 정리가 도움이 된다는 설명과, ⑤ 앞 놀이 공간 간소화의 중요성 사이에서, 간소화가 필요한 이유를 제시한다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Teaching materials actively counters this illusion by requiring deep processing, active recall, and revealing gaps in knowledge.
question_data.Paragraph: Students often mistake familiarity with true mastery, creating a dangerous 'illusion of competence' where recognizing information feels like genuine knowledge. ( ① ) This cognitive bias, strengthened by passive study methods, leads learners to overestimate their understanding. ( ② ) It introduces powerful methods like teaching imaginary students, peer-teaching in study groups, and writing explanations for others. ( ③ ) Ultimately, adopting the teacher mindset transforms surface familiarity into real mastery. ( ④ ) This exposes and fills gaps in knowledge and ensures solid, reliable understanding. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
②
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '가르치기'가 능력 착각에 대항한다는 내용이다. ① 뒤에서 수동적 학습이 이해를 과대평가하게 한다는 문제 제기 직후, 그 해결책으로 제시되며, ③의 구체적 방법 소개로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: In other words, they would represent five fish with a numeral for the number five alongside a symbol for the fish.
question_data.Paragraph: From around 3000 BC, the Sumerian officials would mark their lists of goods on clay tablets. ( ① ) If they wanted to record five fish, they would mark five pictures of a fish. ( ② ) Their first great intellectual leap came from separating the number from the object they were counting. ( ③ ) If they wanted to describe five of something else, they realized they could keep the same numeral and trade the object symbol for a cow, or a jar of oil. ( ④ ) The Sumerians had developed the idea of an emancipated number, existing in its own right and independent of whatever it is being used to count. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 숫자 5의 기호와 물고기 기호를 나란히 놓는 것을 설명한다. ② 뒤에서 '숫자와 세는 대상을 분리한 지적 도약'을 언급한 직후, 그 구체적 방법을 보여주고, ④의 다른 대상에 같은 숫자를 적용하는 것으로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Still, their dislike did not affect their tendency to copy ingroup behavior more closely than outgroup behavior.
question_data.Paragraph: The emphasis on learning from the ingroup takes little account of individuals. ( ① ) Amazingly, while we may dislike some of the people in our ingroup, we still copy them. ( ② ) Ingroups and outgroups were distinguished in a minimal way, using simple color coding of wristbands. ( ③ ) The children did not simply like all members of their ingroup; they disliked those who behaved in an antisocial fashion. Indeed, they liked these antisocial ingroup members less than the prosocial members of an outgroup. ( ④ ) This overimitation reveals that we are not copying in order to learn how to open a box most efficiently. ( ⑤ ) We want to learn how to do it in the proper way ─ conforming to the rules and conventions of the ingroup.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '그럼에도 불구하고 싫어함이 내집단 행동을 더 많이 모방하는 경향에 영향을 미치지 않았다'는 내용이다. ③ 뒤에서 반사회적 내집단 구성원을 싫어했다는 설명 직후, 그럼에도 모방했다는 반전을 제시하고, ⑤의 과잉모방 설명으로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Different people's fat cells will have different set points, and the strength of the message is affected by the number of cells.
question_data.Paragraph: The 'set point theory' concept is that your body has a stable quantity of fat cells by the time you are an adult. ( ① ) The more weight you carried in your childhood and your teenage years, the more fat cells you will have as an adult. ( ② ) These fat cells then become 'fuller' or 'thinner' as you gain and lose weight as an adult. ( ③ ) The set point is the trigger in these cells that will send a message to your brain saying that your fat cells are getting too thin and that you must eat more. ( ④ ) Thus a person with lots of fat cells and a high set point will battle with a strong desire for food when dieting. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 사람마다 다른 세트포인트와 세포 수에 따른 신호 강도를 설명한다. ③ 뒤에서 세트포인트가 뇌에 메시지를 보낸다는 설명 직후, 개인 차이를 설명하고, ⑤의 '지방세포가 많고 세트포인트가 높은 사람'이라는 결론과 연결된다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: But it's important to recognize that the way of thinking that lies behind these words slowly extinguishes certain habits from our daily life.
question_data.Paragraph: Initially the catchphrase 'less is more' had a simple meaning. ( ① ) First mentioned in Robert Browning's poem, it suggests that everything simple is better and more beautiful than the complex and tangled. ( ② ) Nowadays this phrase is heard often ─ maybe even too often. ( ③ ) For example, think about the heavy, massive radio receivers that existed back in the day. ( ④ ) Over time, many of their buttons became viewed as 'extra' and were removed, and with each reduction these devices eventually developed into the phones in our pockets. ( ⑤ ) The scale of the object became smaller, and the functions of the buttons got lost in the three-dot menus.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 이 말 뒤의 사고방식이 일상 습관을 소멸시킨다는 내용이다. ② 뒤에서 이 문구가 자주 들린다고 한 직후, 그 이면의 의미를 설명하고, ④의 구체적 예시(라디오)로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: This dream came with a price.
question_data.Paragraph: We first need to understand the short but hidden history behind the plastics industry. ( ① ) Before plastic, people lived more sustainably, with far fewer waste problems than we face today. ( ② ) However, after the invention of plastic, its rapid adoption during World War II, and its explosion into consumer goods, plastic was everywhere. ( ③ ) The growing industry came to symbolize the convenience and prosperity of the American dream. ( ④ ) Over time, people began to notice the environmental impact, and the early seeds of today's environmental movement were planted. ( ⑤ ) However, the plastics industry was quick to counter these concerns, launching campaigns that presented recycling as the solution.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '이 꿈에는 대가가 따랐다'는 전환 문장이다. ③ 뒤에서 아메리칸 드림의 상징이 되었다고 한 직후, 그 대가를 언급하고, ⑤의 환경 영향 인식으로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: For example, a look into a microscope tells you nothing unless you are already informed about the nature of the instrument and what you are supposed to be looking at.
question_data.Paragraph: As Kuhn proposed, our propositions about the world are embedded within paradigms. ( ① ) Thus, when scientists undertake research, they do so from within a specific paradigm. ( ② ) Even the most exacting measurements are only sensible from within that paradigm. ( ③ ) Thus, what we call major progress in science is not a movement from a less to a more accurate paradigm. ( ④ ) Rather, it is a horizontal shift from one 'way of seeing the world' to another. ( ⑤ ) For Kuhn, 'the scientist with a new paradigm sees differently from the way he had seen before.'
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 현미경을 들여다봐도 도구와 관찰 대상에 대한 사전 지식 없이는 알 수 없다는 예시이다. ② 뒤에서 가장 정밀한 측정도 패러다임 내에서만 의미가 있다는 주장 직후, 그 구체적 예시로 제시된다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Yet a few hours later, after the sun has warmed the greenhouses above freezing, the second visit presents a miraculous contrast.
question_data.Paragraph: Making two visits to one of our cold greenhouses ─ one at dawn after a below-zero night, and the other a few hours later ─ provides a striking introduction to the winter harvest. ( ① ) During the dawn visit all the crops are frozen solid. ( ② ) Raising the inner covers, which is difficult because they too are frozen, reveals a sight of hard, frost-coated leaves bleak enough to convince anyone that this idea is foolish. ( ③ ) Under the inner covers are closely spaced rows of vigorous, healthy leaves that stretch the length of the greenhouse. ( ④ ) The leaf colors in different shades of greens, reds, maroons, and yellows stand bright against the dark soil. ( ⑤ ) It looks like a never-ending spring.
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 햇빛으로 온실이 따뜻해진 후 기적적 대조를 보여준다는 전환이다. ② 뒤에서 새벽 방문의 황량한 모습 직후, 몇 시간 뒤의 대조적 모습으로 전환하고, ④의 싱싱한 잎과 색상 묘사로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: Unlike most political experts, she's not just talking about economic policies like family leave and government-supported childcare.
question_data.Paragraph: Parenting experts say children need to learn independence and resilience. ( ① ) But cities and suburbs don't offer safe walk and bike routes to school, and free time disappears under a spreadsheet of activities. ( ② ) As Darby Saxbe recently wrote, 'underparenting requires structural change.' ( ③ ) She's talking about actual physical structures, and the cultural change required to populate them. ( ④ ) We need to 'build back our tolerance for children in public spaces,' she writes, 'and create safe environments where lightly supervised kids can move around freely.' ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
③
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '대부분의 정치 전문가와 달리'로 시작하여 경제 정책만이 아님을 언급한다. ② 뒤에서 '구조적 변화가 필요하다'고 한 직후, 그 구조적 변화의 범위를 설명하고, ④의 '물리적 구조와 문화적 변화'로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: But as society accelerates, something shifts.
question_data.Paragraph: It's fair to say that patience has a terrible name. ( ① ) For one thing, the prospect of doing anything that you've been told will require patience simply seems unattractive. ( ② ) More specifically, though, it's disturbingly passive. It is the virtue that has traditionally been urged upon housewives, or on racial minorities told to wait for their full civil rights. ( ③ ) In all such cases, patience is a way of psychologically accommodating yourself to a lack of power. ( ④ ) In more and more contexts, patience becomes a form of power. In a world geared for hurry, the capacity to resist the urge to hurry is a way to gain purchase on the world, to do the work that counts. ( ⑤ )
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 '그러나 사회가 가속화되면서 무언가가 변한다'는 전환이다. ③ 뒤에서 인내가 무력에 순응하는 것이라고 한 직후, 사회 변화로 인내의 의미가 달라진다는 전환을 제시하고, ⑤의 '인내가 힘의 형태가 된다'로 이어진다.

question_data.Question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.
question_data.InsertSentence: The king, puzzled and slightly annoyed at what he did, asked, 'What is the meaning of this coin? I am the richest king in the land.'
question_data.Paragraph: One day, a sage was traveling with his disciple through the capital of a prosperous kingdom. ( ① ) As they walked along a busy road, the sage noticed a coin on the ground. Since he lived simply and had no need for it, he told his disciple he hoped to give it to someone who truly required it. ( ② ) The two men searched the streets, but found no one in genuine need of the coin. The next morning, near the palace, they saw the king preparing for a war. ( ③ ) He approached the sage and asked for a blessing that might ensure victory. After a quiet moment of thought, the sage handed the king the coin he had found the previous day. ( ④ ) The sage replied calmly, 'I found this coin yesterday while walking through your capital. I had no need for it, so I planned to give it to someone who did. Yet everyone I met seemed content.' ( ⑤ ) The sage added, 'Today, I see you preparing for war, seeking to conquer more. And I thought you, the one who desires more, were in need of this coin.'
question_data.Options:
① ①
② ②
③ ③
④ ④
⑤ ⑤
question_data.Explanation:
④
*해설: 주어진 문장은 왕이 동전의 의미에 대해 의아해하며 자신이 가장 부유한 왕이라고 말하는 내용이다. ③ 뒤에서 현자가 왕에게 동전을 건넨 직후, 왕의 반응으로 ④에 위치하고, ⑤의 현자의 답변으로 이어진다.
